title: Mass Assignment

explain.what: An API endpoint applies all incoming request fields directly to the database — no allowlist. Fields that should be server-controlled, like is_admin or role, become writable by anyone who crafts the right JSON body.
explain.example: A profile update sends name and bio. Add "is_admin": true to the JSON and the server writes it. There is an auth check — but it never validates which fields are acceptable, or whose profile is being updated.
explain.impact: Privilege escalation · account takeover · unauthorized data modification · hard to spot in code review because the auth check is real

vulnerable.annotation: ⚠ Three subtle issues: (1) req.body.user_id controls the update target, (2) is_admin accepted from the client, (3) no ownership check.

fixed.annotation: ✅ Allowlist only safe fields (name, bio), always target the authenticated user, never accept server-controlled flags from clients.

demoHint: Can you spot all 3 issues before clicking the attack buttons? Look at: who controls the update target? Which fields are accepted? Is there an ownership check?
